title: campanula elatines subsp. glaberrima
intro: Campanula elatines subsp. glaberrima, also known as the alpine bellflower, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the bellflower family (Campanulaceae), native to the Mediterranean region. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Campanula elatines subsp. glaberrima include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a potential natural remedy for various skin conditions and infections. Herbal preparations of Campanula elatines subsp. glaberrima can be made by infusing the plant in hot water or using it in a topical cream or salve, although its use should be under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
